name: C3 AI Mountain Peak Design System
description: Light-first aesthetic. White and Silica backgrounds dominate. Carbon (#1A1A1A) is the darkest value — True Black is eliminated. Dot grid textures overlay light backgrounds and can mask photography. Typeface: Helvetica Neue.

color.description: Core brand palette: Carbon, Tungsten, Graphene, Silica, White, and C3 Red accent. Light-first — White and Silica dominate backgrounds; Carbon is used for text and sparse dark accents (footer, nav). True Black (#000000) is eliminated from the system.

primary.name: White
primary.usage: Primary background, text on dark surfaces

primary.name: Silica
primary.usage: Secondary background, card fills, light sections

primary.name: Carbon
primary.usage: Primary text, dark accents, nav/footer backgrounds

secondary.name: Graphene
secondary.usage: Muted text, secondary labels, supporting content

secondary.name: Tungsten
secondary.usage: CTA buttons, hover states, error indicators, category tags

accent.name: C3 Red
accent.usage: Product-specific use only — not a general UI accent. Reserved for brand mark and specific marketing contexts.

rules: True Black (#000000) is eliminated — Carbon (#1A1A1A) is the darkest value
Extended palette is reserved for product-specific or industry-specific communication only
Silica and Tungsten are used for functional UI elements, hover states, and error indicators
All colors stored as HSL in CSS custom properties

typography.description: Helvetica Neue type hierarchy with Title Case headlines at 100% tracking and sentence case body copy.
rules: All text is left-aligned — no centered text
H1–H4 and buttons use Title Case with normal (0) letter-spacing
H5–H6, body, captions, and pullquotes use Sentence case
Never use all-caps on headlines or buttons
Headlines (H1–H4) use text-transform: capitalize in CSS
Meta uses text-transform: uppercase with 0.08em tracking

buttons.description: Primary, secondary, and ghost button styles with sharp corners (0px radius) and Title Case labels.
rules: All button labels use Title Case with normal letter-spacing
Never use all-caps or sentence case on buttons
Sharp corners — border-radius is always 0

variants.name: Primary
variants.tailwind: bg-carbon text-white type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:bg-tungsten transition-colors

variants.name: CTA
variants.tailwind: bg-tungsten text-white type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:bg-carbon transition-colors

variants.name: Outline
variants.tailwind: border-2 border-carbon text-carbon type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:bg-carbon hover:text-white transition-colors

variants.name: Ghost
variants.tailwind: border border-border text-muted-foreground type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:border-carbon transition-colors

variants.name: On Dark — Primary
variants.tailwind: bg-white text-carbon type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:bg-silica transition-colors

variants.name: On Dark — Outline
variants.tailwind: border-2 border-white text-white type-cta px-8 py-4 font-bold hover:bg-white hover:text-carbon transition-colors

inputs.description: Form inputs, text areas, selects, and search fields styled with the brand system. Sharp corners, no border-radius.
rules: All inputs use sharp corners (0px border-radius)
Error states use Tungsten — not C3 Red
Padding: 12px 16px
Font: system sans, base size

badgesAndLabels.description: Status indicators, tags, and category labels using Carbon backgrounds with white text.
badgesAndLabels.badgeStyle: px-3 py-1, text-xs, font-bold, uppercase, tracking-widest

cards.description: Content cards with sharp corners, structured layout patterns. Three surface variants.

metricCards.description: Grid of stat cards separated by 1px border gaps

rules: Sharp corners — never use border-radius on cards
CTA links use uppercase tracking-widest style
Padding: 32px (p-8)

spacing.description: 8pt grid system with defined spacing tokens from 4px to 192px.
rules: All spacing values are multiples of 8px
Section padding: space-16 (128px) or space-24 (192px)
Component internal padding: space-8 (64px)
Element gaps: space-4 (32px) to space-6 (48px)
Max content width: 1400px (max-w-screen-xl)

styleRules.description: Typography casing rules, alignment standards, and letter-spacing guidelines.

rules.title: Title Case for headlines
rules.description: H1 through H4 are always written in Title Case — every word is capitalised.

rules.title: Title Case for buttons
rules.description: All button labels and calls to action are written in Title Case. Never use all-caps or sentence case on buttons.

rules.title: Sentence Case for body copy
rules.description: All body text, captions, pullquotes, and supporting copy use sentence case.
rules.correct: C3 AI is solving previously unsolvable problems across complex organizations.
rules.incorrect: C3 AI Is Solving Previously Unsolvable Problems Across Complex Organizations.

rules.title: Sentence Case for sub-headlines
rules.description: H5 and H6 sub-headlines use sentence case.
rules.correct: Why this matters for your business
rules.incorrect: Why This Matters For Your Business

rules.title: Always left-aligned
rules.description: All text is left-aligned. There is no centered text in the C3 AI system.

rules.title: Normal letter spacing for headlines
rules.description: Headlines (H1–H4) use normal letter-spacing (letter-spacing: 0). Body copy, sub-headlines, and buttons also use normal tracking.

voiceAndTone.description: Brand voice guidelines: human, sophisticated, aspirational, and intelligent. Tone adapts to context.

toneTenets.name: Human
toneTenets.description: Open and engaging. Our language is relatable without feeling forced or overly familiar. We respect our audience by prioritizing their needs before our own.

toneTenets.name: Sophisticated
toneTenets.description: Confident, refined, and clear. We stay on topic and get to the point. Our language is polished without being formal or self-important.

toneTenets.name: Aspirational
toneTenets.description: Forward-looking and empowering. We don't just talk about the future — we explain how to get there. Our tone makes progress feel achievable today.

toneTenets.name: Intelligent
toneTenets.description: Relevant and responsive. We understand our audience's context and meet them where they are. We sound smart — but never like we know it all.

dos: Connect with and engage your audience
Acknowledge user needs before promoting the brand
Use contractions for a natural, conversational feel (e.g., we're, not we are)
Be concise and remove unnecessary complexity
Prioritize clarity and quality over quantity
Use clear value statements and back up claims with specifics
Use specifics when referencing future technology or innovation
Reinforce messages with clear calls to action
Be proactive — anticipate questions and address context
Assume intelligence, regardless of technical expertise
donts: Lead with self-promotional or boastful language
Use slang or cultural references unfamiliar to a global audience
Use dated or overly formal business language
Talk down to readers or overcomplicate explanations
Speak in vague or hypothetical terms (e.g., 'future-ready' without proof)
Rely on tech or business clichés
Use excessive jargon for non-technical audiences
Use metaphors, puns, or clever phrasing that could confuse

contentStrategy.description: Content hierarchy, messaging frameworks, and copy guidelines. Users should consume content in a predictable, logical flow — from confusion to actionable understanding.

framework.title: Hook / Problem
content: Acknowledge the user's world and context
Clearly define the primary pain points
Make the cost of inaction explicit

framework.title: Solution
content: Introduce the product, service, or offering as the answer
experience: Answer the core user problem early
Provide CTAs that invite deeper exploration

framework.title: Value Proposition & Benefits
content: Explain how the solution addresses the problem
Highlight tangible outcomes (time saved, revenue gained, risk reduced)
experience: Expand with supporting data and context
Reframe ideas in practical, relatable terms

framework.title: Primary Call to Action
content: Drive toward a meaningful next step (demo, trial, consultation)
experience: Show how the offering fits real workflows

framework.title: How It Works
content: Explain the mechanics of the offering
Show how it fits within an existing stack

framework.title: Logistics & Implementation
experience: Distill next steps into clear, low-friction actions

contentStrategy.corePrinciple: Every piece of content should help move the user from confusion to actionable understanding.

imagery.description: Photography style, image treatments, and visual content guidelines. Two styles: Systems at Scale and Subject in Focus.

styles.name: Systems at Scale
styles.description: Illustrates scale and complex systems. Geometric compositions with visual rhythm, strong lines, textures, and patterns that communicate vastness.

styles.name: Subject in Focus
styles.description: Humanistic and intimate, focusing on a single subject. Evocative but simple, direct, and dynamic. Strong perspective adds impact.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Industrial Scale
preferredImageLibrary.description: Reactor vessel under construction inside a vast steel-paneled assembly facility.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Precision Machinery
preferredImageLibrary.description: Welder alongside a robotic arm with green laser on a large curved metal component.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Aerospace Defense
preferredImageLibrary.description: B-21 stealth bomber centered in a darkened hangar, straight-on silhouette.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Global Supply Chain
preferredImageLibrary.description: Cargo ship being loaded with an aircraft fuselage section by crane.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Aerospace Manufacturing
preferredImageLibrary.description: Airbus A321 assembly line inside a pristine factory floor.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Naval Industry
preferredImageLibrary.description: Shipyard at sunset with large cranes reflected in still water.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Ship Construction
preferredImageLibrary.description: Rusted ship hull block with scaffolding ladders and pipes at dusk.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Space Infrastructure
preferredImageLibrary.description: NASA Vehicle Assembly Building interior — towering steel lattice.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Data Infrastructure
preferredImageLibrary.description: Data center interior with vibrantly colored cooling pipes and cable trays.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Precision Processing
preferredImageLibrary.description: Industrial printing press rolls photographed from above — strong rhythm and pattern.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Power Generation
preferredImageLibrary.description: Massive industrial turbine rotor with intricate blades showing immense scale.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Advanced Manufacturing
preferredImageLibrary.description: Automated welding robot on a large circular metal component with green laser guidance.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Cleanroom Operations
preferredImageLibrary.description: Two technicians in full cleanroom suits inspecting a monitor.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Campus Life
preferredImageLibrary.description: Team members walking outdoors on campus stairs with coffee.

preferredImageLibrary.label: Scientific Research
preferredImageLibrary.description: Researcher examining equipment in a laboratory with complex machinery.

usageRules: Photography should always be immersive and engaging
Panoramic imagery, closely cropped frames, and densely populated visuals work well
Selected imagery should be naturalistic and real
treatments: Dot masks can reveal photography through multi-scale circular cutouts
Gradient overlays (carbon to transparent) for text legibility on hero images
Grayscale is not required — full color is standard

animation.description: Two primary animation patterns: Cascade Reveal and Parallax Drift. Motion is applied intentionally — not natively — to components when a design calls for it.

patterns.name: Cascade Reveal
patterns.description: Elements fade and translate into position as the user scrolls them into the viewport.
patterns.usage: Wrap content in <CascadeReveal variant='text'>, add 'cascade-child' class to each staggered element.

patterns.name: Parallax Drift
patterns.description: Full-viewport sections where the background image moves at a slower rate than the foreground content, creating depth.

rules: Motion is opt-in, not default
One well-timed hero animation creates more delight than scattered micro-interactions
Cascade Reveal is the primary scroll animation for all content sections

dots.description: Dot grid textures and patterns — the lifeblood of the C3 AI brand. Provides texture, rhythm, and visual interest. Dots serve as subtle background textures on light surfaces and can mask photography.

scales.usage: Dense texture, data metaphors, background patterns
Prominent patterns, section dividers, focal elements
Maximum scale — single focal circles, rarely used

backgroundTexture.description: Uniform small dot grid as subtle background texture on light sections
backgroundTexture.css: background-image: radial-gradient(circle, hsl(var(--carbon)) 1px, transparent 1px); background-size: 20px 20px; opacity: 0.10;

compositions.name: Staircase
compositions.description: Multi-scale cascade — progressive dot sizes suggest growth and transformation

compositions.name: Multi-Scale Grid
compositions.description: Mixed density composition — small grids alongside large focal circles

compositions.name: Dense Grid
compositions.description: Uniform dense grid — represents raw data, complexity, and scale

compositions.name: Left Anchor
compositions.description: Edge-anchored pattern — partial grid for asymmetric layouts and bleeds

compositions.name: Top Anchor
compositions.description: Top-anchored pattern — horizontal dot band for headers and section dividers

compositions.name: Halftone Grid
compositions.description: Multi-scale halftone grid — progressive dot sizes create depth and visual rhythm

compositions.name: Ascending Clusters
compositions.description: Clustered dot formations — ascending scale suggests growth and emergence

animations.name: Grid Reveal
animations.description: Sequential dot appearance with staggered fade — loading states and data visualization reveals

animations.name: Radial Pulse
animations.description: Breathing wave emanating from center — data processing or system analysis in progress

animations.name: Circle Formation
animations.description: Dots in circular arrangement with traveling highlight — cyclical processes and continuous operation

animations.name: Scale Cascade
animations.description: Center-out scale animation — expanding influence or insight radiating from a central point

animations.name: Convergence
animations.description: Dots converge to center and disperse — data aggregation and synthesis of information

animations.name: Wave Columns
animations.description: Traveling wave across columns — data flow, streaming information, or sequential processing

animations.name: Diagonal Sweep
animations.description: Curtain-like reveal along the diagonal axis — transitions, section reveals, and cinematic entrances

animations.name: Diamond Morph
animations.description: Grid dots pulse into a diamond formation — structured transformation and data crystallization

animations.name: Spiral Orbit
animations.description: Dots trace expanding spiral paths — growth, discovery, and expanding reach of intelligence

usageRules: Create metaphors — use dots to visually represent complex systems, data networks, or AI processes
Complement text — dots work alongside typography to reinforce messaging
Build dynamic compositions — combine multiple dot scales for visual interest
Respect the grid — all compositions follow strict mathematical relationships
avoid: Don't use dots as decorative noise — every pattern should have intention
Don't use colors outside the C3 palette (Carbon, Graphene, White) for dots
Don't rotate or skew dot grids — always align to the baseline grid
Don't use True Black (#000000) for dots — Carbon (#1A1A1A) is the darkest value

components.description: Composite UI components built on the C3 AI design system. Swiss-grid system, brand color palette, 8pt spacing grid.

library.name: WebNav
library.description: Primary site navigation with logo, inline links, and outlined CTA button.

library.name: HeroFullscreen
library.description: Full-viewport hero with optional background image. Light (default) and dark variants.
variants: light (dot grid texture, dark text on silica)
dark (background image, white text on carbon)

library.name: HeroHomepage
library.description: Homepage hero with bold/light headline split, play button, and tagline.

library.name: Features4x
library.description: Dark-background 4-column feature grid with numbered cards, icons, and a tag pill header.

library.name: Features6x
library.description: Dark-background 3×2 feature grid with bordered cells, numbered cards, icons, and optional CTA.

library.name: Features3xHero
library.description: Large hero heading with tag and description, plus 3 image feature cards below.

library.name: FeaturesGrid
library.description: Flexible feature grid supporting 3-column, 2×2, and 3×2 layouts with optional hero image.

library.name: FeatureSplit
library.description: Two-column layout with text on one side and image on the other.

library.name: GalleryMosaic
library.description: Full-width masonry-style image grid with hover caption overlay on dark background.

library.name: GalleryTiled
library.description: Equal-size tile grid with numbered hover overlay, arrow icon, and slide-up label.

library.name: Gallery
library.description: Horizontally scrolling image carousel with prev/next arrows and dot indicators.

library.name: ContactSplit
library.description: Dark-background split layout: left headline with contact details, right form with underline inputs.

library.name: ContactForm
library.description: Light-themed contact section on silica with white input fields and carbon submit button.

library.name: CtaCentered
library.description: Dark-background centered CTA with large headline, two buttons, and social proof badges.

library.name: CtaSplit
library.description: Split layout: light left panel with copy and CTA, dark right panel with background image and pricing card.

library.name: ContentCardsTiled
library.description: Asymmetric bento-grid layout: large hero card spanning rows with smaller cards alongside.

library.name: ContentCards5050
library.description: Headline with two equal side-by-side image cards, each with a label overlay.

library.name: AccordionSection
library.description: Expandable accordion on silica background with semantic text colors.

library.name: RequestDemoFooter
library.description: Silica-background section with bold headline and carbon CTA button.

library.name: MegaFooter
library.description: Full-width mega footer with branded headline, staircase motif, categorized link columns, and bottom legal bar.

library.name: CascadeReveal
library.description: Scroll-triggered animation wrapper. Applies cascade-reveal animations to children.

library.name: ParallaxDrift
library.description: Full-viewport parallax section with background image depth effect.

icons.description: Google Material Symbols (Outlined) loaded via Google Fonts. Icons render as ligatures.

tailwindConfig.description: Tailwind CSS configuration extending the design system tokens.

globalCSSRules.description: Global CSS rules applied via index.css base layer.
rules: All elements use border-border by default
Body uses bg-background text-foreground with antialiased font smoothing
All text elements default to text-align: left
h1-h4 elements have font-weight: 700, letter-spacing: 0, text-transform: capitalize
h5-h6 elements have font-weight: 700, letter-spacing: normal, text-transform: none
